pi_items.source: setup_opencad.sh lines 222: User 'James Rodriguez' created via PHP PDO with explicit email 'james.rodriguez@opencad.local'. setup_task.sh line 14 queries this exact email to get user ID.

setup_opencad.sh line 234 comments specify department_id 5 is 'Police'. setup_task.sh lines 15,35 queries department_name='Police' and inserts into user_departments. export_result.sh line 26 checks for removal of Police access.

setup_opencad.sh line 234 comments specify department_id 8 is 'Civilian'. setup_task.sh lines 16,36 queries department_name='Civilian' and inserts into user_departments. export_result.sh line 29 verifies Civilian access is retained.
setup_opencad.sh line 217: Admin user created via PHP PDO with email 'admin@opencad.local'. Line 360 confirms login credentials. Task description uses this email for login.

setup_opencad.sh line 216: password_hash('Admin123!', PASSWORD_DEFAULT) creates bcrypt hash for admin user. Line 360 confirms credentials: 'admin@opencad.local / Admin123!'. Task description specifies this password.

privileged_info_summary: James Rodriguez (james.rodriguez@opencad.local) is a test user in the OpenCAD system created by setup_opencad.sh. He is assigned to both the Police and Civilian departments via the setup_task.sh script. The task requires revoking his Police department access while preserving Civilian access. Administrator login is admin@opencad.local with password Admin123!. The department names match official OpenCAD schema definitions: Police (ID 5) and Civilian (ID 8).
